summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the second part of the 'Whatever It Takes' series with a passionate call to action for 2026. He declared that a 'whatever it takes' mentality is not a slogan but a line in the sand that will cost something, perhaps everything. The central message was a battle cry to 'burn the bridges' in our lives—to decisively cut off all pathways back to our old, sinful nature. Using the powerful example from Acts 19, where new believers publicly burned their valuable sorcery books, Pastor Travis illustrated that true freedom comes not from quiet repentance but from a public, fiery declaration of being done with the past. He emphasized that as long as a bridge remains, we will be tempted to cross back over it. The sermon focused on three specific bridges every believer must burn: the Access Bridge (people, places, and patterns that provide easy return to sin), the Identity Bridge (old labels and self-perceptions that contradict who God says we are), and the Security Bridge (the backup plans and counterfeit comforts we trust more than God). He concluded by pointing to the only bridge that should remain: the cross of Jesus Christ, which is the true bridge to salvation, identity, and security. The call was for total surrender, moving from a life of half-measures to being all in for Jesus, trusting that when we burn our man-made bridges, God's supernatural provision and true freedom are revealed.
mainPoints: True freedom and spiritual breakthrough require burning the bridges—the people, places, and patterns—that provide easy access back to our old sinful life.
We must burn the bridge of our old identity, rejecting the labels and mindsets of our past to fully embrace who God says we are as new creations in Christ.
We must burn the bridge of false security, surrendering our backup plans and control to fully trust in God as our only source of provision and safety.

versesMentioned.text: Many who became believers confessed their sinful practices. A number of them who had been practicing sorcery brought their incantation books and burned them at a public bonfire. The value of the books was several million dollars. So the message about the Lord spread widely and had a powerful effect.

If your right eye causes you to stumble, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell. And if your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to go into hell.
With such nagging she prodded him day after day until he was sick to death of it.

In the spring, at the time when kings go off to war, David sent Joab out with the king’s men and the whole Israelite army. They destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah. But David remained in Jerusalem.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!

Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.
Abraham answered, 'God himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering, my son.' And the two of them went on together.

Monday.scripture: Acts 19:18-20 (NIV) - '...they burned them at a public bonfire.'
Monday.action: Identify one tangible item, digital file, or contact that represents an 'access bridge' to your past. Make a plan to delete, discard, or distance yourself from it this week.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to make a decisive break from anything that pulls me away from You. Help me to value my freedom in Christ more than my comfort.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 5:29-30 (NIV) - 'If your right eye causes you to stumble, gouge it out...'
Tuesday.action: Take the step you planned yesterday. Delete the app, block the number, or throw away the item. Do not just hide it; remove the access point.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to cut off access to sin today. I trust that Your grace is sufficient and that holiness is worth any temporary loss.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 (NIV) - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come...'
Wednesday.action: Write down an old label or negative identity you carry (e.g., 'broken,' 'addict,' 'failure'). Then, write God's truth about you from Scripture (e.g., 'forgiven,' 'new creation,' 'beloved'). Pray over the old label and symbolically destroy the paper.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank You that I am not who I used to be. I receive my new identity in Christ today. Help me to forget what is behind and live as the person You say I am.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - 'Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead...'
Thursday.action: When a thought of past failure or shame arises today, consciously reject it. Speak aloud or in your heart: 'I am forgetting what is behind. I press on toward Christ.'
Thursday.prayer: God, my past does not define me. I choose to leave it at the foot of the cross today. Fill my mind with thoughts of Your future for me.

Friday.action: Identify one area where you rely on a 'backup plan' more than on God (e.g., a financial safety net you hoard out of fear, a relationship you cling to for security). Take one step of trust, such as giving a gift of faith or having a difficult conversation in reliance on God.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I confess I often trust my plans more than Your provision. Today, I surrender my security to You. I believe You will provide everything I truly need.

Saturday.scripture: John 8:36 (NIV) - 'So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Celebrate one bridge you took a step to burn. Share this victory with a trusted friend or family member to solidify your commitment.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for setting me free. Help me to walk in that freedom every day, burning any new bridges the enemy tries to build. My only security, identity, and access is found in You.
